purpose: Route ambiguous web-project requests to the narrowest child across informational sites, fullstack web applications, and browser games.
selection_order: Browser games beat other web work when the core request involves gameplay loops, Three.js/WebGL, Canvas, levels, physics, or game-specific UI.
Fullstack web applications beat informational sites when the request needs ongoing user workflows, app state, auth, dashboards, admin surfaces, ecommerce behavior, or backend logic.
Default to informational sites when the main need is publishing content, presenting a brand, or driving conversion without app-style product behavior.

children.name: game
children.summary: Builds browser games and real-time interactive experiences with game-specific rendering, asset, and QA guidance.
route_when: The request is about a browser game, gameplay loop, level design, HUD, Three.js/WebGL, Canvas, physics, or game-specific assets and controls.
The main output is an interactive game experience rather than a business site or app workflow.
avoid_when: The request is for a dashboard, SaaS product, admin panel, ecommerce workflow, or other app-style interface.
The request is for a content-first website, landing page, blog, portfolio, or small-business site.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover website-building children automatically.

children.name: webapp
children.summary: Builds fullstack or app-like web products with structured UI, state, backend patterns, and dashboard guidance.
route_when: The request is for a SaaS product, dashboard, admin panel, authenticated workflow, ecommerce app, or any site with significant app state or backend logic.
The main output is an interactive product workflow rather than a content-first site or browser game.
avoid_when: The request is fundamentally a browser game or real-time playable experience.
The request is a simple portfolio, landing page, editorial site, or brochure site with no app-style behavior.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover website-building children automatically.

children.name: informational
children.summary: Builds content-first websites such as portfolios, editorial sites, landing pages, blogs, and small-business pages.
route_when: The request is for a portfolio, editorial site, blog, landing page, brochure site, personal site, or small-business website.
The main output is content presentation, brand communication, or conversion without app-style workflows or gameplay.
avoid_when: The request needs dashboards, auth, backend flows, persistent app state, or ecommerce product behavior.
The request is for a browser game, game loop, or real-time playable experience.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover website-building children automatically.
